questions.prompt: In `public boolean loginSucceeded(WebDriver driver)`, what is `WebDriver driver`?
options: The return type
A parameter — an input the caller passes in
The method name
A field of the class
questions.explanation: The part in parentheses is the parameter list. boolean is the return type and loginSucceeded is the name.

questions.prompt: Which statement correctly distinguishes a class from an object?
options: A class is a single instance; an object is the blueprint
A class is the blueprint; an object is a concrete instance built from it with new
They are the same thing
An object can exist without any class
questions.explanation: The class defines the structure (fields and methods); each object is an instance created from that blueprint, with its own field values.

questions.prompt: What is the job of a constructor?
options: To return a boolean for assertions
To compile the class
To run when an object is created with new, setting up its initial state
To delete an object from memory
questions.explanation: A constructor shares the class name, has no return type, and runs at creation time to initialize the object's fields.

questions.prompt: Why is it useful for a check like loginSucceeded(...) to return a boolean?
options: Because booleans run faster than other types
So the result can be passed straight into an assertion such as assertTrue(...)
Because methods must always return boolean
So the method does not need parameters
questions.explanation: A boolean return feeds directly into assertTrue/assertFalse, keeping the test readable and the logic centralized in one method.

questions.prompt: Given `LoginPage login = new LoginPage("/login");`, what does `new` do?
options: Declares the class blueprint
Creates a new object (instance) of LoginPage and runs its constructor
Calls the open() method
Compiles the LoginPage class
questions.explanation: new allocates a fresh object from the class and invokes the constructor, which here stores "/login" in the url field.
